rxn_class_summary: electrochemical oxidative radical 1,2-halofunctionalization (iodo-, bromo-, chloro-) of alkenes with carboxylic acids, water, or alcohols as nucleophiles using nBu4NX as the halogen source
electrochemical_role: anodic oxidation of halide (X-) from nBu4NX to halogen radical (X•), which adds to the alkene; the resulting carbon radical is further anodically oxidized to a carbocation that is trapped by the nucleophile. An alternate path involves anodic oxidation of X• to X+ which forms a haliranium ion intermediate.

key_intermediates.name: halogen radical X• (I•, Br•, Cl•) generated at anode
β-halo carbon radical (intermediate I) from X• addition to alkene
β-halo benzylic carbocation (intermediate II) from anodic oxidation of I
haliranium / halonium ion (intermediate III) from X+ addition to alkene

electron_flow: Halide anion X- is preferentially oxidized at the anode (lower Eox than the alkene) to give X•, which adds to the alkene to form a benzylic C-radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is captured by the carboxylate/water/alcohol nucleophile. In parallel, X• can be further oxidized to X+ which forms a haliranium ion attacked by the nucleophile. At the cathode, H+ is reduced to H2.

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (spin trap)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals consistent with iodine and bromine radicals detected during electrolysis of nBu4NI and nBu4NBr; DMPO adducts also observed in model reactions with 1a/1b

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane 2a (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: With nBu4NI only cyclopropyl-retained product 54 (56%) was obtained (radical oxidized to cation before ring opening, or I- oxidized to I+); with nBu4NBr both retained 55 (12%) and ring-opened 56 (40%) formed; with nBu4NCl retained 57 (20%) and ring-opened dichloro 58 (32%) formed, supporting radical intermediates Int-2 and Int-3

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of nBu4NI, nBu4NBr, nBu4NCl vs 1-methoxy-4-vinylbenzene and benzoic acid
probe_reagents.observation: Eox(nBu4NI)=+0.373 V, Eox(nBu4NBr)=+0.686 V, Eox(nBu4NCl)=+0.815 V vs SCE in MeCN/DCM, all much lower than 1a (+1.413 V); benzoic acid shows no oxidation up to +3.0 V — halides are preferentially oxidized

notes: Two parallel pathways are proposed in Scheme 4: (a) radical addition of X• to alkene then anodic oxidation of the C-radical to carbocation II (radical–polar crossover), and (b) anodic oxidation of X• to X+ followed by haliranium ion III formation; both are quenched by the nucleophile. The cyclopropyl-retained product with iodide could indicate either I- → I+ pathway or fast oxidation of the radical to a cation before ring opening, so the iodination case does not unambiguously distinguish the two routes.
